description: GitOps home operations repository for a single-node Talos Kubernetes homelab cluster. The project aims to build enterprise-grade self-hosted infrastructure, maintain future-proof infrastructure skills, and replace big-tech/subscription services with privacy-respecting alternatives.

goals.description: Develop enterprise-grade homelab infrastructure using GitOps, Talos, Kubernetes, Flux, and strong operational practices.
Maintain useful future infrastructure, security, automation, and self-hosting skills through practical operation of the cluster.
Replace big-tech and subscription services with self-hosted, privacy-respecting, low-lock-in alternatives where practical.
Keep the single-node cluster understandable, recoverable, observable, and safely change-managed.

architecture.pattern: Infrastructure-as-code GitOps monorepo for a single-node Talos Kubernetes cluster.

modules.name: Talos
modules.description: Talos and talhelper configuration and patches

modules.name: Bootstrap
modules.description: Bootstrap Helmfile and encrypted bootstrap material

modules.name: Tasks/scripts
modules.description: Operational command wrappers and bootstrap helpers

architecture.dataFlow: Local declarative manifest changes are validated, reviewed, committed to Git, and then applied by Flux after push/merge. HelmRelease resources pull charts from HelmRepository/OCIRepository sources. Secrets are provided through SOPS or External Secrets backed by 1Password Connect.

workflows.name: development
workflows.description: Direct-to-main local workflow with self-review gate and manual commit/push/apply decisions.
steps: commit/push only after explicit user approval

workflows.name: GitOps apply
workflows.description: Flux applies committed repository state to the cluster after push/merge.

workflows.name: secret management
workflows.description: Prefer ExternalSecret + 1Password Connect for new app credentials; avoid secret decryption and raw Secret commits.

workflows.name: cluster-affecting operations
workflows.description: Live cluster/Talos/bootstrap commands are gated by explicit confirmation.

processes.name: Plan-first infrastructure change
processes.description: Research chart/app, inspect repo patterns, draft plan, ask decisions, implement only after sufficient clarity.

processes.name: Safe GitOps app deployment
processes.description: Add namespace/app manifests following existing Flux/Kustomize/ExternalSecret conventions and validate locally.

processes.name: Secret-safe troubleshooting
processes.description: Diagnose without decrypting or printing secrets unless explicitly confirmed.

painPoints.description: Secret handling spans SOPS, age, 1Password Connect, and External Secrets; unsafe reads/decrypts could leak credentials.
painPoints.suggestedRemediation: Keep strict confirmation gates and prefer ExternalSecret for new app credentials.

painPoints.description: Some Kubernetes correctness depends on cluster state even when local rendering passes.
painPoints.suggestedRemediation: Run kustomize/kubeconform/flux-local locally where possible and use live commands only with confirmation.

painPoints.description: Single-node cluster means storage and workload changes can have outsized operational impact.

bottlenecks.description: Peacesmp HelmRelease is high-churn and may benefit from careful validation/review.
Mixed SOPS and ExternalSecret patterns require careful secret strategy selection.
Taskfile exposes live and destructive cluster/Talos operations.

naming.secrets: ExternalSecret preferred for new credentials; *.sops.yaml only when explicitly requested

git.commitPrefixes: feat:, fix:, chore: without scope unless asked

codeStyle.yaml: preserve document starts, concise, pinned versions
codeStyle.shell: strict mode and existing helper patterns

conventions.errorHandling: Explain risks and ask before destructive/live/secret operations; keep blockers in-progress rather than pretending success.
conventions.testingConventions: Prefer safe local validation: kustomize build, kubeconform, flux-local; do not run live cluster commands without confirmation.
additionalRules: Do not decrypt or reveal secrets
Do not commit raw Kubernetes Secret values

claudeMdInstructions: Follow AGENTS.md as the source of truth for repository safety and workflow.
For Kubernetes changes, inspect existing app patterns before editing and prefer ExternalSecret for new credentials.
Do not run live cluster, Talos, bootstrap, or secret decryption commands without explicit confirmation.
Use direct, structured, precise summaries and call out risks/placeholders.
